page: Microsoft Azure D4plds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Dpldsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores utilizing the Microsoft Neoverse-N2 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz with one thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Storage is provided via a 236 GB local disk, and the instance is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-threaded gzip compression capabilities, reaching 33.06 GB/sec. Multi-core and single-core stress-ng CPU evaluations fall within average ranges, with scores of 8275.96 and 2074.79 respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show an average read-only small block cached speed of 453.63 GB/sec, though uncached large block write speeds drop to 39.96 GB/sec. Weaknesses are observed in cryptographic tasks, with the Passmark CPU encryption test scoring in the bottom 10% at 741.37, and ray tracing performance is also weak.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D4plds_v6 offers a streamlined, low-memory hardware profile that reduces resource overhead for compute-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints. The dedicated ARM architecture provides predictable execution without hyperthreading interference. This configuration is suitable for workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, text processing, and lightweight database operations.
description: Microsoft Azure D4plds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Dpldsv6 family, powered by 4 dedicated Microsoft Neoverse-N2 physical cores running at 3.4 GHz. It features a compute-oriented hardware profile with 8.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core) and a 236 GB local disk. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers strong single-threaded gzip compression performance alongside average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth capabilities. However, it exhibits lower performance in cryptographic operations and ray tracing. The dedicated ARM architecture and low memory-to-CPU ratio make it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads that prioritize dedicated compute resources over large memory footprints. It is well-suited for applications such as static web serving, software compilation, text processing, and lightweight database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4plds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 8 GB of RAM, and a 236 GB local disk, optimized for compute-focused workloads.

tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine featuring dedicated physical cores and local storage for compute-focused workloads requiring minimal memory overhead.

8.0 GB of system memory and 236 GB of local storage
Strong single-threaded gzip compression performance of 33.06 GB/sec
Qualitatively cost-efficient compute-to-memory ratio of 2.0 GB per core
Best suited for static web serving, software compilation, and text processing
